content:
✨ awesome-android-root | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To serve as a centralized, curated, and easily navigable resource for discovering and learning about Android rooting tools, modules, and guides.
✨ Key Features: • Curated list of 500+ root apps and modules • Support for Magisk, KernelSU, and LSPosed (Xposed)
📖 Summary: Awesome Android Root is a comprehensive resource hub for Android rooting enthusiasts. It curates over 500 top root apps, Magisk, KernelSU, and LSPosed modules, along with detailed rooting guides for various devices. The repository aims to be the ultimate destination for discovering and managing tools and information related to rooting Android devices.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Reversecore_MCP | HTML
🎯 Primary Use Case: To empower AI agents and security professionals to conduct sophisticated reverse engineering, malware analysis, vulnerability research, and digital forensics by translating natural language queries into automated, multi-tool analysis workflows.
✨ Key Features: • AI-Powered Security Analysis • Integrated Static & Dynamic Analysis • 50+ Analysis Tools (Radare2, YARA, LIEF, Capstone, etc.)
📖 Summary: Reversecore MCP is an enterprise-grade server that integrates over 50 analysis tools to enable AI agents to perform advanced security tasks like reverse engineering, malware analysis, and vulnerability research through natural language commands. It acts as a bridge, translating human intent into complex, multi-tool security analysis workflows.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 skills | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide developers with ready-to-use components (skills) that enhance AI agents, allowing them to interact with and leverage Google Cloud services and other Google technologies more efficiently.
✨ Key Features: • Pre-built Agent Skills for Google products and technologies • Integration with Google Cloud services (e.g., Gemini API, BigQuery, GKE, Firebase) • Modular installation via `npx skills add`
📖 Summary: This repository provides a collection of pre-built 'Agent Skills' designed to integrate with Google products and technologies, particularly Google Cloud services. These skills enable developers to easily add complex functionalities to their AI agents, streamlining development and interaction with Google's ecosystem.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 whichllm | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To help users identify and select the most performant local LLM that can run effectively on their specific computer hardware, bypassing simple parameter count metrics.
✨ Key Features: • Auto-detects hardware (GPU/CPU/RAM) • Ranks LLMs from HuggingFace based on real benchmarks and hardware fit • Supports various installation methods (uvx, pip, brew) • Allows simulation of different hardware configurations
📖 Summary: whichllm is a command-line tool that helps users find and run the best-performing Large Language Models (LLMs) locally on their specific hardware. It benchmarks models based on real-world performance and hardware compatibility, rather than just parameter count, providing a ranked list of suitable LLMs.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
